summary: The Docker build failed because a RUN step that wget'ed the nydus binary from GitHub returned a 502 Bad Gateway and exited with code 1, causing buildx bake to fail.
root_cause: A wget invocation failed with 'HTTP/1.1 502 Bad Gateway or Proxy Error' when downloading nydus-static v2.4.0 from GitHub, and the RUN command exited with code 1 which aborted the build.

evidence.reason: Shows the exact build step executing wget to download the nydus release.

evidence.quote: wget: server returned error: HTTP/1.1 502 Bad Gateway or Proxy Error
evidence.reason: Demonstrates the network/remote-server error returned by wget (502 Bad Gateway).

evidence.quote: ERROR: failed to solve: process "/bin/bash -c wget https://github.com/dragonflyoss/nydus/releases/download/$NYDUS_VERSION/nydus-static-$NYDUS_VERSION-$TARGETOS-$TARGETARCH.tgz" did not complete successfully: exit code: 1
evidence.reason: Confirms the wget failure caused the Docker build to fail with a non-zero exit code.
Identifies the Dockerfile line where the failing RUN command is located.

evidence.quote: ##[error]buildx bake failed with: ERROR: failed to solve: process "/bin/bash -c wget https://github.com/dragonflyoss/nydus/releases/download/$NYDUS_VERSION/nydus-static-$NYDUS_VERSION-$TARGETOS-$TARGETARCH.tgz" did not complete successfully: exit code: 1
evidence.reason: CI-level failure message showing buildx bake aborted due to the wget failure.

suggested_fix: Retry the workflow (transient network/remote-server errors often resolve). If it continues failing, make the Dockerfile step more resilient (add retries/timeouts), provide a cached/fallback copy of the nydus binary, or update the download source/URL to a reachable location.

summary: A single functional test failed because pip could not download a test package from GitHub due to repeated HTTP 502 errors (network/server error).
root_cause: The test tests/functional/test_truststore.py::test_no_truststore_can_install[GitHub] failed because pip could not fetch https://github.com/pypa/pip-test-package/archive/refs/heads/master.zip; the run produced an OSError from an HTTPSConnectionPool after repeated 502 error responses.

evidence.reason: Identifies the failing test that exercised a GitHub URL.
Shows the test attempted to download a package directly from GitHub.

evidence.quote: ERROR: Could not install packages due to an OSError: HTTPSConnectionPool(host='github.com', port=443): Max retries exceeded with url: /pypa/pip-test-package/archive/refs/heads/master.zip (Caused by ResponseError('too many 502 error responses'))
evidence.reason: Concrete error message indicating the download failed due to repeated 502 responses from GitHub, causing an OSError.
The test harness reports the pip invocation exited with a non-zero return code, causing the test to fail.

suggested_fix: This appears to be a transient network/server error. Retry the CI run first. To make tests more robust, avoid relying on GitHub being reachable in functional tests—serve the test package from a local test server or fixture/mirror, or add retry/backoff logic in the test when downloading external URLs.

summary: The CI failed because one Jest test suite failed: a test in test/config/get.ts failed, with logs showing a warning about an environment-variable replacement (${ENV_VAR_123}).
root_cause: A Jest test in test/config/get.ts failed (at test/config/get.ts:32:38); the logs show a warning '[WARN] Failed to replace env in config: ${ENV_VAR_123}', indicating an environment-variable replacement issue surfaced during that test.

evidence.quote: Test Suites: 1 failed, 3 skipped, 61 passed, 62 of 65 total
evidence.reason: Shows that one test suite failed overall in the Jest run.
Stack trace pinpoints the failing test location to test/config/get.ts line 32.

evidence.quote: [WARN] Failed to replace env in config: ${ENV_VAR_123}
evidence.reason: Log message indicates an environment variable replacement failure that occurred during the test run.
The test run exited with a non-zero status, consistent with a failing test suite.

suggested_fix: Ensure the environment variable used by the config is provided or mocked for the test: set ENV_VAR_123 in the CI job environment before running tests, or update test/config/get.ts to supply or mock the variable (or to tolerate/suppress missing env-var warnings).
